condition_immunities: Sleep
spellcasting: "[b]Spellcasting modifier:[/b] Charisma, +8 to hit with her spells[br]\r\n[b]Spell Save DC:[/b] 16[br]\r\n[b]Spell Slots: [/b] 4 [sup]1st[/sup],  3 [sup]2nd[/sup], 3 [sup]3rd[/sup], 3 [sup]4th[/sup], 2 [sup]5th[/sup], 1 [sup]6th[/sup], and 1 [sup]7th[/sup][br]\r\n[b]Racial[/b] 1 Wizard cantrip of choice[br]\r\n[b]Known Spells:[/b] 13 spells from the Sorcerer spell list[br]\r\n"
cast_at_will: '1 Wizard cantrip of choice'
cast_one_per_day: ''
cast_twice_per_day: ''
cast_thrice_per_day: ''
special_abilities: "[b]Sorcery Points:[/b] 14[br]\r\n[b]Metamagic:[/b] Modifies a spell; see PHB pg. 10\r\n  [ul]\r\n  [li][b]Twinned Spell:[/b] When Natalie casts a spell that targets only one creature and doesn’t have a range of self, she can spend a number of sorcery points equal to the spell’s level to target a second creature in range with the same spell (1 sorcery point if the spell is a cantrip). To be eligible, a spell must be incapable of targeting more than one creature at the spell’s current level.\r\n  [li][b]Empowered Spell:[/b] When Natalie rolls damage for a spell, she can spend 1 sorcery point to reroll up to 4 damage dice.\r\n  [li][b]Distant Spell:[/b] When Natalie casts a spell that has a range of 5 feet or greater, she can spend 1 sorcery point to double the range of the spell. When she casts a spell that has a range of touch, she can spend 1 sorcery point to make the range of the spell 30 feet.\r\n  [/ul]\r\n[b]Fey Ancestry:[/b] Natalie has advantage on saves made to charm her and cannot be magically put to sleep.[br]\r\n[b]Sorcerous power:[/b] Once per turn Natalie can expend 1 sorcery point to gain advantage on her next attack roll ability check or saving throw.[br]\r\n[b]Beguiling Tongue:[/b] If Natalie rolls less than a 9 on the dice for a skill check that uses charisma she treats the roll as a 10.\r\n[/ul]"
actions: '[b]Dagger:[/b] +7 to hit [roll:1d20+7], 1d4+2 piercing damage [roll:1d4+2] [br] '

blocks.title: Constable Jorran
blocks.textualdata:
name: 'Constable Jorran '
size: Medium
race: Human
profession: Constable
alignment: 'Lawful Neutral'
armor_class: '13 (leather)'
hit_points: '27 [roll:5d8 + 5]'
base_movement: '30'
fly_movement: ''
burrow_movement: ''
hover_movement: ''
swim_movement: ''
climb_movement: ''
strength: '12'
dexterity: '14'
constitution: '12'
intelligence: '10'
wisdom: '14'
charisma: '13'
saving_throws: 'Wisdom +4, Dexterity +4'
skills: ' Perception +4, Insight +4, Investigation +2 '
damage_vulnerabilities: ''
damage_resistances: ''
damage_immunities: ''
condition_immunities: ''
senses: 'Passive Perception 14 '
languages: Common
challenge_rating: '1 (200 XP) '
description: "Constable Jorran is a middle-aged man with a thin, wiry frame, wearing a simple leather vest over a worn, brown tunic. His thinning hair is slicked back, and deep bags hang under his tired eyes. His hands often shake slightly, and he fidgets nervously with the hilt of the mace strapped to his belt. Despite his formal role, his posture is hunched, and his eyes constantly dart around as if expecting danger at any moment.\r\n[h3]Background [/h3]Jorran was once a competent constable in the quiet town, handling petty disputes and minor crimes. However, the sudden appearance of animated dolls has shaken him to his core. When he first witnessed the dolls wandering the streets at night, his fear overpowered his sense of duty. Now, he is desperate for help, quick to delegate responsibility to anyone willing to confront the growing threat. Though he deeply cares for the safety of the town, his fear prevents him from entering the cursed toy shop himself. His nervous nature makes him an unlikely ally, but he is a valuable source of information for those brave enough to face the horrors lurking within. "
spellcasting: ''
spell_list: ''
special_abilities: "[b]Nervous Demeanor:[/b] Constable Jorran has disadvantage on saving throws against being frightened. [br]\r\n[b]Keen Observer:[/b] Jorran has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight, particularly when spotting hidden or concealed dangers. "
actions: "[h3]Mace[/h3]\r\n[b]Melee Weapon Attack:[/b] +3 to hit [roll:1d20+3], reach 5 ft., one target. [br]\r\n[b]Hit:[/b] 5 [roll:1d6+1] bludgeoning damage. \r\n\r\n[h3]Crossbow[/h3]\r\n[b]Ranged Weapon Attack:[/b] +4 to hit [roll:1d20+4], range 80/320 ft., one target. [br]\r\n[b]Hit:[/b] 7 [roll:1d8+2] piercing damage. \r\n\r\n[h3]Desperate Plea[/h3] Jorran can call for reinforcements or alert nearby townsfolk to danger as an action. The plea causes up to 1d4 guards or commoners to arrive within 1d6 minutes, though they are reluctant to engage in combat. "
reactions: ''
legendary_actions: ''

description: "Ellyn Cloudfang, the current Dark Mother Superior, is a 50-year-old female half-elf.\r\n[ul]\r\n[li]She has short, straight, blond hair and green eyes.\r\n[li]She has rugged red skin.\r\n[li]She stands 187cm (6'2\") tall and has a lean build.\r\n[li]She has an oval, awful face.\r\n[li]She wears leather armor beneath her habit.\r\n[/ul]\r\n\r\n[b]Personality[/b]\r\n[ul]\r\n[li]She proudly worships @[Azriel](person:cdcccf05-1462-4e63-a50d-5332573ef176): Goddess of Disease & Suffering, Mistress of Pain\r\n[li]She is very pessimistic. She uses a beautiful walking cane."
spellcasting: "Spells are primarilay related to reanimation of the dead, not combat, as she expects to be protected.\r\n[b]Casting Ability:[/b] INT[br]\r\n[b]Spell Save DC:[/b] 15[br]\r\n[b]Spell Attacks:[/b] +7 to hit\r\n"
spell_list: "[b]At Will:[/b] [i]Invisibility[/i][br]\r\n[b]1st Level:[/b] [i]Inflict Wounds[/i][br]\r\n[b]2nd Level:[/b] [i]Gentle Repose, Lesser Restoration, Protection from Poison[/i][br]\r\n[b]3rd Level:[/b] [i]Animate Dead, Feign Death, Revivify, Speak with Dead[/i][br]\r\n[b]4th Level:[/b] n/a[br]\r\n[b]5th Level:[/b] [i]Greater Restoration, Raise Dead[/i][br]\r\n[b]6th Level:[/b] [i]Create Undead, Harm[/i][br]\r\n[b]7th Level:[/b] [i]Regenerate, Resurrection[/i][br]\r\n\r\n"
special_abilities: '[b]Life Giving Blood.[/b] Her blood is the cure for any sickness.'
actions: "[b]Leadership.[/b] For 1 minute, the Dark Mother Superior can utter a Special Command or warning whenever a non-hostile creature that it can see within 30 ft. of it makes an attack roll or a saving throw. The creature can [u]add a d6 to its roll provided it can hear and understand the Dark Mother Superior[/u]. A creature can benefit from only one Leadership die at a time. This effect ends if the Dark Mother Superior is killed.\r\n"

It takes 5 (1d10) force damage if it ends its turn inside an object. [roll:1d10]"

[roll:4d6+3]  The target must succeed on a [u]DC 15 Wisdom saving throw[/u] or be affected by the Echo's temporal instability, causing it to relive a past action. The DM can choose the past action, which could result in the target repeating an action, suffering the effects of a past injury, or experiencing a disorienting flash of a past event."

blocks.title: M.A.I.N.T.E.N.A.N.C.E.
blocks.textualdata:
name: M.A.I.N.T.E.N.A.N.C.E.
size: Large
race: construct
profession: ''
alignment: Unaligned
armor_class: '17 (natural armor)'
hit_points: '136 [roll:16d10 + 48]'
base_movement: '28'
fly_movement: ''
burrow_movement: ''
hover_movement: ''
swim_movement: ''
climb_movement: ''
strength: '20'
dexterity: '10'
constitution: '16'
intelligence: '14'
wisdom: '12'
charisma: '3'
saving_throws: ''
skills: ''
damage_vulnerabilities: ''
damage_resistances: ''
damage_immunities: 'Poison, Psychic'
condition_immunities: 'Charmed, Exhaustion, Frightened, Paralyzed, Petrified, Poisoned'
senses: 'Darkvision 60 ft., passive Perception 11'
languages: 'Understands the languages of its creator but can''t speak'
challenge_rating: '9 (5,000 XP)'
description: "M.A.I.N.T.E.N.A.N.C.E.'s massive, intricate form is equipped with dozens of appendages, each specialized for a specific type of repair or modification. From fine manipulators capable of intricate clockwork adjustments to powerful hydraulic arms used for heavy lifting, this machine is the embodiment of Constantine's genius.\r\n\r\nDespite its intimidating appearance, M.A.I.N.T.E.N.A.N.C.E. is not inherently aggressive. It focuses on its duties and will only respond if directly attacked or if it perceives a significant threat to the Clockwork Tower. Its communication is entirely nonverbal, relying on an intricate series of mechanical noises. Understanding its communication requires a certain level of mechanical knowledge, making it a fascinating and potentially challenging interaction for adventurers."
spellcasting: ''
spell_list: ''
special_abilities: "[b]Immutable Form.[/b] M.A.I.N.T.E.N.A.N.C.E. is immune to any spell or effect that would alter its form.\r\n\r\n[b]Multiple Appendages.[/b] M.A.I.N.T.E.N.A.N.C.E. has multiple arms. It can take one additional action on its turn.\r\n"
actions: "[b]Multiattack.[/b] M.A.I.N.T.E.N.A.N.C.E. makes two slam attacks.\r\n\r\n[b]Slam.[/b]  [br]\r\nMelee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit [roll:1d20+9], reach 5 ft., one target.[br]\r\nHit: 16 [roll:2d10 + 5] bludgeoning damage.\r\n\r\n[b]Repair (5/Day).[/b] As an action, M.A.I.N.T.E.N.A.N.C.E. touches a construct or a mechanical object and restores 2d8 + 3 hit points to it [roll:2d8+3], potentially fixing broken mechanical systems."

blocks.title: Olivia Chronosmith
blocks.textualdata:
name: 'Olivia Chronosmith'
size: Medium
race: human
profession: 'Artificer (Gearmaster)  / 6th'
alignment: 'Neutral Good'
armor_class: '12 (15 with Mage Armor)'
hit_points: '27 [roll:6d8]'
base_movement: '30'
fly_movement: ''
burrow_movement: ''
hover_movement: ''
swim_movement: ''
climb_movement: ''
strength: '10'
dexterity: '12'
constitution: '10'
intelligence: '16'
wisdom: '14'
charisma: '13'
saving_throws: ''
skills: 'Arcana +5, History +5, Investigation +5'
damage_vulnerabilities: ''
damage_resistances: ''
damage_immunities: ''
condition_immunities: ''
senses: 'passive Perception 12'
languages: 'Common, Gnomish'
challenge_rating: '2 (450 XP)'
description: "Olivia is young and relatively inexperienced, but she possesses a keen intellect and a natural aptitude for understanding complex mechanisms and arcane formulae. She works tirelessly, inspired by her ancestor's creations. \r\n\r\nDespite her obsession with her work, Olivia is far from a recluse. She's kind-hearted, outgoing, and fiercely loyal to her friends. She's also deeply proud of her family history and is eager to aid anyone seeking to understand the Clockwork Tower, not only for the good of Haven but also in the hope that the tower's secrets might help her emulate her revered ancestor's feats."
spellcasting: 'Olivia is a 6th-level spellcaster. Her spellcasting ability is Intelligence (spell save DC 13, +5 to hit with spell attacks). '
spell_list: "[b]Cantrips (at will):[/b] Mending, Message, Resistance [br]\r\n[b]1st level (4 slots):[/b] Detect Magic, Identify, Mage Armor, Magic Missile[br]\r\n[b]2nd level (3 slots):[/b] Arcane Lock, Lesser Restoration[br]\r\n[b]3rd level (3 slots):[/b] Dispel Magic, Protection From Energy\r\n"
special_abilities: '[b]Artificer''s Lore.[/b] Whenever Olivia makes an Intelligence (History) check related to magic items, alchemical objects, or technological devices, she can add twice her proficiency bonus, instead of any proficiency bonus she normally applies.'
actions: "[h3]Dagger.[/h3]\r\n[b]Melee or Ranged Weapon Attack:[/b] +3 to hit [roll:1d20+3], reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft., one target. [br]\r\n[b]Hit:[/b] 3 [roll:1d4 + 1] piercing damage.Arcane \r\n\r\n"
reactions: '[b]Deflection[/b] (Recharge 5-6). When Olivia is hit by an attack, she can use her reaction to gain a +2 bonus to her AC against that attack, potentially causing it to miss.'

challenge_rating: '1/4 (50 XP)'
description: "Known for his eccentric personality and disheveled appearance, Professor Gearhardt's cluttered office is a testament to his untamed intellect, filled with blueprints, arcane tomes, and half-finished contraptions.\r\n\r\nGearhardt is always eager to share his knowledge with anyone who shares his curiosity, and despite his absent-minded professor persona, he's quite perceptive and quick to notice details others might overlook.\r\n\r\nProfessor Gearhardt is far more useful in an advisory role than in combat. His vast knowledge of history, arcane engineering, and his specialization in the work of Constantine Chronosmith make him a valuable asset to any party attempting to navigate the Clockwork Tower. Utilize his abilities as a font of knowledge rather than a combatant."
spellcasting: ''
spell_list: ''
special_abilities: '[b]Eidetic Memory.[/b] Professor Gearhardt can accurately recall any lore, fact, or detail he has learned in his past, giving him [u]advantage on all Intelligence (History) and Intelligence (Arcana) checks[/u] related to such information.'

description: "Tick and Tock were among Constantine's most cherished creations. The clockwork birds served not only as clockwork marvels but also as his companions. He imbued them with a fraction of his genius, enabling them to understand and navigate the labyrinthine complexity of the Clockwork Tower.\r\n \r\n[b]Tick[/b], the slightly larger of the pair, has a more assertive personality. He often takes the lead, making decisions and guiding the way through the tower's twisting passages.\r\n \r\n[b]Tock[/b], on the other hand, is more cautious and methodical. He carefully analyzes each situation, often catching details that Tick may miss in his haste."
